title: `kube generate` prints in Note: text, which text is not necessary applicable for the current case
body:
### Issue Description

`podman generate kube myname` prints:

```
# NOTE: If you generated this yaml from an unprivileged and rootless podman container on an SELinux
# enabled system, check the podman generate kube man page for steps to follow to ensure that your pod/container
# has the right permissions to access the volumes added.
```

Podman shall determine if it is run privileged, rootful, under SELinux -enabled system.  Based on its fidings, podman shall  adapt (shorten) the Note-message above.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

See above

### Describe the results you received

See above

### Describe the results you expected

See above

### podman info output

```yaml
# podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.4.3-dev
API Version:  4.4.3-dev
Go Version:   go1.19.3
Git Commit:   73ce3c61c1bcf22a26f0442f23bf19c2cfc24a82
Built:        Wed Mar  1 21:58:11 2023
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```


### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@umohnani8 Not sure why this message is in here?  The SELinux handling would be required for either rootful or rootless systems?  The UserNamespace handling is just for rootful, I believe.  Should we make Podman smarter when generating the message?

This message was introduced by https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/a8b3c67b974bd1b55a80188826e2c7ca983a9d95 because we had some issues around users not being able to access their volumes in a k8s cluster from the generated yaml and that was because of some permissions configured by podman on SELinux enabled systems that we couldn't translate to the generated yaml.
There are a few options the user has to make the volume accessible and we couldn't automatically decide for them so that is why we have a note in the generated yaml and have steps in the man pages on what needs to be done.
I think the ask here is to make is smarter such that it only generates on a SELinux enabled system, is there a way for podman to know that?
